title: 10 best herbal capsules for dry cough
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for dry cough are a natural remedy designed to provide relief from persistent dry, hacking coughs. These capsules typically contain a blend of herbs such as slippery elm, licorice root, and thyme, which help soothe and calm the throat, reducing inflammation and irritation. Examples of herbal capsules that help with dry cough include Bronchium Forte and Mucinex Natural, which have been shown to provide quick and effective relief from symptoms, allowing individuals to breathe easier and sleep more soundly. By using these capsules, individuals can improve their overall quality of life by reducing the discomfort and disruption caused by persistent dry cough.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with dry cough because they contain glycyrrhizin, a natural compound that soothes and calms irritated throats. The capsule's mucilages help to coat the throat, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with dry coughs. Additionally, licorice root has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce congestion and irritation in the respiratory tract, providing relief from persistent coughing. As a result, herbal licorice capsules provide effective and natural relief for dry cough symptoms.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Helps to soothe and protect the mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and irritation in the throat, which can contribute to a dry cough.
Flavonoids: Have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce swelling in the airways and calm the cough reflex, providing relief from a dry cough.
Saponins: Act as expectorants, helping to thin and loosen mucus in the lungs, making it easier to expel and relieving congestion that can contribute to a dry cough.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a natural expectorant that helps loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it effective in soothing dry cough symptoms.
Barks: Containing flavonoids and saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the throat and bronchial tubes, providing relief from dry cough discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried licorice root powder from high-quality sources for its anti-inflammatory properties.
Measure out 250 milligrams of powdered licorice root and mix with 50 milliliters of distilled water in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over medium heat for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally, to create a decoction of glycyrrhizin.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into clean glass bottles and let it cool down to room temperature.
Fill gelatin capsules with the cooled decoction and seal them immediately to preserve potency and shelf life for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint capsules helps with dry cough because of its natural ability to soothe and calm the irritated throat and chest, providing fast relief from annoying symptoms. The menthol in peppermint has a cooling effect that loosens mucus and reduces inflammation, making it easier to expel phlegm and relieve congestion. Additionally, peppermint's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce the irritation and discomfort associated with dry cough, allowing for more comfortable breathing and a better night's sleep.
remedy_properties: Menthol: Menthol has natural expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, providing relief from dry coughs.
Eucalyptol: Eucalyptol has anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties, which help to reduce swelling in the throat and nasal passages, easing congestion and coughing.

Volatile Oils: The volatile oils present in peppermint capsules have a soothing effect on the mucous membranes, calming irritation and inflammation in the throat and airways, providing relief from dry coughs and sore throats.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, peppermint roots help soothe dry coughs by reducing inflammation in the throat.
Buds: Containing high levels of menthol and other essential oils, peppermint buds provide quick relief from dry cough symptoms by loosening mucus and relieving congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality peppermint leaves from a trusted source ensuring proper identification.
Dry the fresh leaves in a warm dark place for at least 24 hours or using a food dehydrator.
Grind dried peppermint leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix one part ground peppermint with one part gelatin powder filling size zero capsules according to manufacturer's instructions.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme capsules helps with dry cough because they contain the natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties of thyme, which soothe and calm the mucous membranes in the throat and lungs. The capsules work to thin out mucus, making it easier to expel, while also reducing inflammation and irritation caused by dry air and environmental allergens. As a result, herbal thyme capsules can provide quick relief from dry cough symptoms, offering a natural and effective solution for temporary respiratory discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antitussive: Thyme contains compounds like thymol, which helps to soothe and calm coughing by reducing inflammation in the respiratory tract, making it less irritating and allowing for easier expectoration of mucus.
Expectorant: The volatile oils present in thyme, such as cineole, help to loosen and clear out excess mucus and phlegm from the airways, making it easier to cough up and relieving congestion.

Antiviral and antibacterial: Thyme's antimicrobial properties can help to combat infections that may be contributing to the dry cough, such as viral or bacterial bronchitis or pneumonia, by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and viruses.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots contain compounds like rosmarinic acid, which helps to reduce inflammation and congestion in the respiratory tract, relieving symptoms of dry cough.
Buds: Thyme buds possess flavonoids and phenolic acids that have natural expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, providing relief from dry cough.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh or dried thymus vulgaris leaves at dawn on a sunny day.
Dry the thymus vulgaris leaves in a well-ventilated area for 24 hours.
Grind 50g of dried thymus vulgaris leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix the powdered thymus vulgaris with 1000mg of gelatin and 500ml of distilled water to create capsules.
Fill 100 empty capsules with the mixture and store in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion capsules helps with dry cough because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the irritated mucous membranes in the throat. The capsules contain a combination of antioxidants and vitamins that help to reduce inflammation, making it easier to expectorate mucus and relieve congestion. Additionally, dandelion's diuretic properties can help to thin out mucus, making it easier to cough up and providing relief from persistent dryness and irritation.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and irritation in the respiratory tract, alleviating dry cough symptoms.
Mucilage-rich: The capsule's mucilage content coats the throat and lungs, soothing and protecting them from further irritation, reducing coughing fits and discomfort.

Expectorant: Dandelion's expectorant properties help loosen and expel mucus and phlegm from the airways, relieving congestion and making it easier to clear out debris that may be contributing to dry cough.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the dandelion plant have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing a dry and irritated throat.
Seeds: Dandelion seeds contain mucilages, which help to thicken mucus and provide relief from dry cough symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers in quantities of 20g for 50 capsules.
Grind the dried herb into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered herb with 500mg of silica gel to enhance flowability and reduce clumping.
Fill 50 empty hard-shell gelatin capsules with approximately 400-450mg of the herb mixture.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow capsules helps with dry cough because they contain mucilages, which are natural soothing agents that coat and protect the mucous membranes in the throat. When taken orally, these capsules release a gentle film of mucilage that lubricates and calms irritated tissues, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with dry coughs. The soothing properties also help to thin mucus, making it easier to expel and relieving congestion, allowing for a more effective and efficient recovery from a dry cough.
remedy_properties: Mucilage content: Marshmallow root contains a thick, protective mucilage that helps to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the throat, reducing inflammation and discomfort caused by dry cough.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The flavonoids and saponins present in marshmallow root have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and irritation in the throat and airways, alleviating symptoms of a dry cough.

Soilution and lubrication: Marshmallow root's mucilage helps to solubilize and lubricate the mucus in the respiratory tract, making it easier to expel and reducing congestion, which can contribute to the persistence of a dry cough.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilages that help soothe and protect the throat, making them ideal for treating dry coughs.
Rhyzomes: Containing anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and irritation in the respiratory tract, easing discomfort caused by dry cough.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh marshmallow root althaea officinalis for drying.
Grind the dried marshmallow root into a fine powder using mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground marshmallow root with other herbs of choice such as thyme and licorice.
Fill empty vegetable capsules with the herbal mixture using a scoop.
Store the filled herbal althaea officinalis capsules in an airtight container away from light and heat.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mullein capsules helps with dry cough because they contain a natural anti-inflammatory agent that soothes and calms irritated airways, reducing coughing fits. The herb's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus, easing congestion and coughs. Additionally, mullein has been shown to have antitussive properties, helping to reduce the frequency and severity of dry, hacking coughs. By targeting these underlying causes, herbal mullein capsules provide natural relief from dry, persistent coughing.
remedy_properties: Mucilages: Mullein contains mucilages, which are thick, protective substances that soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes, providing relief from dry, scratchy throats and reducing inflammation.

Saponins: The saponin content in mullein capsules has natural expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear out mucus, bronchial secretions, and phlegm, allowing for easier expulsion and alleviating congestion and coughing.

Antioxidants: Mullein's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the respiratory tract, which can contribute to chronic dry coughs by reducing the body's natural defense mechanisms and protecting against cellular damage.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, which helps to soothe and calm the irritated tissues in the throat, relieving dry cough symptoms.
Leaves: Contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the airways and ease coughing.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried verbascum thapsus leaves and stems and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the ground herb with 250 grams of tapioca starch and 50 grams of gelatin in a bowl for 5 minutes.
Add 100 milliliters of distilled water to the mixture and stir until it forms a smooth paste within 5 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with one gram each of the herb-starch-gelatin paste, using a capsule filler machine or spoon for 10 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months before use or refrigerate for longer storage for 20 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsules helps with dry cough because they contain a unique compound called podophyllotoxin, which has natural expectorant properties. This compound helps to loosen and clear out mucus from the airways, providing relief from persistent dry coughs. Additionally, the capsules also possess anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe the irritated mucous membranes in the throat, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with dry cough.
remedy_properties: Antitussive effect: The capsule's flavonoids, particularly quercetin, have a soothing and calming action on the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and relieving dry cough symptoms.

Expectorant properties: Ginkgo biloba's terpenes, such as sesquiterpene lactones, help loosen and remove excess mucus from the airways, making it easier to expel and alleviating congestion associated with dry coughs.
Anti-inflammatory activity: The capsule's flavonoids and terpenes also have anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling in the lungs and airways, which can contribute to dry cough relief.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and other bioactive compounds, roots have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, making them suitable for dry cough relief.
Barks: Containing a high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids, barks are valued for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help alleviate dry cough symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of fresh ginkgo biloba leaves in the morning when dew still clings to their surface.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator on low heat for 6 hours or until brittle.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 teaspoon of the powdered ginkgo biloba with 250ml of boiling water to create an infusion and let it steep for 15 minutes.
Fill empty capsules with the cooled infusion mixture, adjusting dosages as needed and store in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender capsules helps with dry cough because of their soothing properties, which can ease inflammation in the throat and airways. The antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds present in lavender help to calm irritated tissues, reducing the severity of a dry cough. Additionally, the capsules' calming effects on the nervous system can also promote relaxation, making it easier to fall asleep despite persistent coughing. As a result, English lavender capsules can provide natural relief from dry cough symptoms, helping to improve overall quality of life.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, help reduce inflammation in the throat and airways, which can alleviate symptoms of dry cough.

Expectorant properties: The camphor and borneol present in lavender capsules help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to expel, thereby relieving congestion and dryness associated with a dry cough.

Anxiolytic and calming effects: Lavender's relaxing and soothing properties can help reduce anxiety and stress, which are common triggers of dry coughs, and promote relaxation, allowing for improved sleep and reduced symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the English lavender plant are used due to their soothing properties, which help to calm the dry, irritated throat and reduce coughing.
Seeds: Lavender seeds are used in medicinal capsules because they contain antioxidants that help to reduce inflammation in the lungs and soothe a dry, scratchy throat.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in bulk weighing about 100 grams.
Grind the dried flowers into fine powder using a mortar and pestle for about 30 seconds.
Mix one teaspoon of the ground lavandula angustifolia powder with 200ml of boiling water to create a decoction taking five minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean bowl removing any solids taking two minutes.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with approximately 0.2 grams of the strained lavandula angustifolia powder and seal immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese date capsules helps with dry cough because they contain a unique blend of herbs that soothe and calm the respiratory tract. The capsules' key ingredients, such as licorice root and mulberry leaf, work together to reduce inflammation and ease congestion, allowing for easier breathing and relief from persistent dry coughing. Additionally, the capsules' natural expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus, providing rapid and effective relief from coughs caused by dry air or allergies.
remedy_properties: Antitussive: The capsules contain ingredients like dates, licorice root, and honey, which have anti-tussive properties that help soothe and calm a dry cough, reducing its frequency and severity.

Mucilages: The mucilage content in the capsules, particularly from plant sources like dates and slippery elm, helps to moisten and protect the mucous membranes, alleviating inflammation and irritation caused by dry coughs.

Expectorant: The herbal ingredients in the capsules, such as ephedra and menthol, help to thin and loosen mucus, making it easier to expel and relieve congestion and coughing associated with a dry cough.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help soothe and calm dry cough symptoms.
Rhyzomes: With its natural expectorant properties, rhyzomes aid in loosening and removing mucus from the airways.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality ziziphus jujuba fruits in autumn when ripe and store them in an airtight container.
Dry the harvested fruits using shade or sun drying method for 7-10 days to maintain their nutritional value.
Grind 100g of dried ziziphus jujuba fruits into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground powder with 500mg of glycerin and 200ml of distilled water to create a liquid extract, allowing it to steep for 2 hours.
Fill gelatin capsules with 250-300mg of the herbal extract and seal them tightly after filling each capsule in preparation for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese peony capsules helps with dry cough because they contain the extract of Peonia lactiflora, a natural herb that has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries. The capsules help to soothe and calm the respiratory tract, relieving congestion and inflammation that can exacerbate dry coughs. By reducing inflammation and promoting expectoration, Chinese peony capsules can help to break up mucus and provide quick relief from dry, hacking coughs. Regular use of these capsules may also help to reduce the severity and frequency of dry cough episodes over time.
remedy_properties: Antitussive property: Chinese Peony capsules have antitussive properties, which helps to relieve and reduce the frequency of dry coughing by suppressing the cough reflex.

Expectorant property: The capsules have expectorant properties, which helps to loosen and remove phlegm and mucus from the lungs and airways, relieving congestion and discomfort associated with dry coughs.

Anti-inflammatory property: Chinese Peony capsules have anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to reduce inflammation in the throat and lungs, thereby alleviating irritation and discomfort caused by dry coughs.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and flavonoids, which help soothe and calm the respiratory tract, making them effective against dry cough.
Rhyzomes: Containing starch and fiber, they help to thicken mucus and facilitate its expulsion from the airways, providing relief for dry cough.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried paeonia lactiflora root and grind it into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine the powdered paeonia lactiflora root with 250 milliliters of boiling water in a heat-resistant container and steep for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another container, discarding the solids, and let it cool to room temperature over 2 hours.
Transfer the cooled liquid to a clean glass jar and add 500 milliliters of vodka or glycerin as a preservative, shaking well for 5 minutes to mix.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250-300 milligrams of the liquid extract mixture and store them in an airtight container in a cool, dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary capsules helps with dry cough because of its natural expectorant properties that help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs. The herb's essential oils, such as camphor and borneol, have anti-inflammatory effects that soothe the throat and reduce irritation caused by dry air or allergens. Additionally, rosemary's antimicrobial properties combat respiratory infections that can trigger coughing. By taking herbal rosemary capsules, individuals can naturally alleviate dry cough symptoms and promote a healthier respiratory system.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Rosemary's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as carnosic acid, help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract and alleviate the discomfort associated with dry cough.

Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial properties of rosemary, including its ability to inhibit the growth of bacteria and viruses, can help combat the underlying infection or irritation that is causing the dry cough.
Expectorant properties: Rosemary's expectorant compounds, such as camphor and borneol, help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, providing relief from congestion and coughing fits.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rosemary roots are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm irritated throats.
Leaves: Fresh or dried rosemary leaves contain essential oils that ease respiratory congestion and reduce the severity of dry coughs.
Roots: Rosemary roots are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm irritated throats.
Barks: The bark of rosemary contains flavonoids that act as natural expectorants, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves from a trusted source or store-bought quantity.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 50g of the ground leaves with 500ml of boiling water to create an herbal infusion for 20 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container discarding solids after 10 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250mg of the infused liquid and seal immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder capsules helps with dry cough because they contain natural expectorants that help loosen and clear out mucus from the airways, providing quick relief from congestion. The herbs in elder capsules also have anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the throat and reduce irritation, making it easier to breathe and speak comfortably. Additionally, elder capsules' antiviral and antibacterial compounds may help combat infections that can cause dry coughs, further reducing symptoms and promoting a faster recovery.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Elderberry's antiviral properties help to reduce the severity and duration of a dry cough by inhibiting the replication of viruses such as the common cold and flu, thereby alleviating symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in elder capsules, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help to reduce inflammation in the throat and airways, which can contribute to a dry cough and make it more uncomfortable.

Mucilaginous properties: Elderberry's mucilage content helps to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the throat and airways, providing relief from dryness and irritation that can accompany a dry cough.
remedy_parts: Roots: Elder roots are rich in saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm a dry, irritated cough.
Rhyzomes: The rhizome of the elder plant contains bioactive compounds like phenolic acids and flavonoids, which have expectorant properties to help relieve congestion and ease dry coughing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality sambucus nigra flowers and leaves in late summer or early fall when they are in full bloom.
Dry the sambucus nigra plant material in a warm oven at 60°c for 24 hours or until brittle to preserve its medicinal properties.
Grind the dried sambucus nigra into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle to increase its surface area.
Mix 500 milligrams of the powdered sambucus nigra with 200 milliliters of water in a small bowl to create a smooth paste that can be filled into capsules.
Fill gelatin capsules with the prepared sambucus nigra paste and seal them immediately to preserve their potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules helps with dry cough because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Curcumin, a compound found in turmeric, has been shown to reduce inflammation in the lungs and throat, providing relief from dry cough symptoms. Roots: Containing bioactive compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which have natural expectorant properties that help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs.
remedy_recipe: Measure accurately 500mg of dried turmeric powder from curcuma longa root.
Add 250mg of powdered ginger and 125mg of licorice root powder to the mixture.
Mix the powders thoroughly for at least 30 seconds to ensure uniform blend.
Fill empty vegetable capsules with the blended powder using a capsule filler machine.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container protected from light and moisture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel capsules helps with dry cough because they contain a unique combination of compounds that work together to soothe and calm the mucous membranes in the throat, relieving dryness and irritation. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves in the morning when dew is still present on the leaves for maximum potency.

Crush 50 grams of dried foeniculum vulgare leaves into fine powder using a mortar and pestle to enhance extraction efficiency.
Mix the powdered foeniculum vulgare with 100 grams of gelatin capsules in a bowl, filling each capsule with approximately 250 milligrams of the herbal mixture.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to six months or refrigerate for longer storage.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh verbena officinalis leaves and flowers of known identity and purity.
Dry the harvested plant material in a well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator at 60 degrees fahrenheit for 24 hours.
Grind the dried herb into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or spice grinder to ensure uniform texture.
Mix 1 part powdered verbena officinalis with 2 parts carrier medium such as gelatin capsules or silica dioxide and fill into #0 vegetarian capsules.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months and consume 3-4 capsules twice daily with water.

remedy_recipe: Gather dried cistus x incanus leaves and flowers in amounts of 20 grams per capsule.
Grind the cistus x incanus mixture into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle within 15 minutes.
Combine 200mg of ground cistus x incanus with 100mg of excipient material such as silica or calcium carbonate.
Fill #00 gelatin capsules with the prepared herbal blend using an encapsulation machine within 10 seconds.
Store the finished capsules in a cool dark place for up to six months to maintain potency.

Grind dried eucalyptus leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 500mg of ground eucalyptus powder with 100mg of organic rice flour and fill capsules using a capsule machine.

The capsules stimulate the production of mucus in the lungs, which helps to loosen and clear out congestion, providing relief from dry, hacking coughs. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of milkweed help to reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing discomfort associated with a dry cough.
remedy_properties: Saponins: Saponins have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the airways, loosen mucus, and ease congestion, making it easier to expel phlegm and soothe a dry cough.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory actions, which can help protect the lungs from oxidative stress and inflammation caused by irritants, further reducing coughing fits and discomfort.

Alkaloids: Alkaloids, such as lappaconitine and delphinine, have antispasmodic properties that can relax the smooth muscle in the airways, reducing constriction and spasms that often exacerbate dry coughs.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm irritated throats.
Seeds: Contain mucilage, a thick, protective substance that coats and protects the throat, reducing irritation and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality asclepias syriaca leaves and flowers in the early morning ensuring maximum potency and freshness.
Dry the plant material using shade drying or oven drying at low heat for 24 hours to preserve nutrients.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or spice grinder for optimal extraction.
Mix 500mg of the powdered asclepias syriaca with 250mg of tapioca starch and fill capsules using a capsule machine within 30 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months or freeze for longer preservation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian capsules helps with dry cough because it contains compounds that have a soothing effect on the respiratory system, reducing inflammation and relieving irritation in the throat. The herb's natural anti-inflammatory properties help to calm the cough reflex, making it easier for the body to expel excess mucus and phlegm, thereby providing relief from dry, scratchy throats. Additionally, valerian's calming effects can also promote relaxation and reduce stress, which are known triggers of coughing fits.
remedy_properties: Sedative properties: Valerian's sedative effects can help to calm the body and promote relaxation, which can help reduce the severity of dry cough symptoms by decreasing irritation in the throat.

Expectorant properties: Valerian's expectorant properties can help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to expel and relieving congestion and discomfort associated with dry cough.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Valerian's anti-inflammatory effects can help to reduce inflammation in the throat and lungs, which can exacerbate dry cough symptoms and make them more difficult to manage.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in volatile oils and sesquiterpenes, the roots are often used to create a soothing expectorant effect that helps loosen mucus in the chest, relieving symptoms of dry cough.

Rhyzomes: The rhizome's high concentration of valepotriates, a type of glycoside, has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, making it effective in reducing coughing spasms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh valerian root at dawn or before noon to ensure optimal potency.
Cleanse the harvested roots by washing them thoroughly with cool water and mild soap.
Dry the cleaned roots in a warm well-ventilated area for 7-10 days or until completely brittle.
Crush the dried valerian root into small pieces using a mortar and pestle then sift through a fine-mesh sieve to ensure uniform size.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with approximately 500mg of crushed valeriana officinalis powder per capsule and seal.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat dry cough with herbal capsules is to combine them with steam inhalation. Herbal capsules containing ingredients like licorice root, slippery elm, and marshmallow root help soothe the throat and reduce inflammation. Taking 2-3 capsules twice a day with warm water or tea can bring relief from dry cough symptoms. Additionally, inhaling steam from a bowl of hot water mixed with eucalyptus oil or menthol can further ease congestion.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to dry cough that are treated with herbal capsules are bronchitis, asthma, and pharyngitis. These conditions often manifest as recurring or persistent coughs, making herbal supplements an effective natural remedy. Capsules containing herbs like Thyme, Goldenseal, Echinacea, and Slippery Elm help soothe the respiratory tract, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms such as congestion and wheezing.
